2604.06788.title: From Perception to Autonomous Computational Modeling: A Multi-Agent Approach
2604.06788.paper_summary: We present a solver-agnostic framework in which coordinated large language model (LLM) agents autonomously execute the complete computational mechanics workflow, from perceptual data of an engineering component through geometry extraction, material inference, discretisation, solver execution, uncertainty quantification, and code-compliant assessment, to an engineering report with actionable recommendations. Agents are formalised as conditioned operators on a shared context space with quality gates that introduce conditional iteration between pipeline layers. We introduce a mathematical framework for extracting engineering information from perceptual data under uncertainty using interval bounds, probability densities, and fuzzy membership functions, and introduce task-dependent conservatism to resolve the ambiguity of what `conservative' means when different limit states are governed by opposing parameter trends. The framework is demonstrated through a finite element analysis pipeline applied to a photograph of a steel L-bracket, producing a 171,504-node tetrahedral mesh, seven analyses across three boundary condition hypotheses, and a code-compliant assessment revealing structural failure with a quantified redesign. All results are presented as generated in the first autonomous iteration without manual correction, reinforcing that a professional engineer must review and sign off on any such analysis.
2604.06788.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個與求解器無關的框架，其中協調的大型語言模型（LLM）代理自主執行完整的計算力學工作流程，從工程組件的感知數據到幾何提取、材料推斷、離散化、求解器執行、不確定性量化以及符合代碼的評估，最終生成包含可行建議的工程報告。代理被形式化為在共享上下文空間中的條件運算符，並設有質量閘以在管道層之間引入條件迭代。我們引入了一個數學框架，用於在不確定性下從感知數據中提取工程信息，使用區間界限、概率密度和模糊隸屬函數，並引入依賴於任務的保守性，以解決當不同極限狀態受對立參數趨勢支配時「保守」的含義的模糊性。該框架通過一個有限元素分析管道進行演示，應用於一張鋼製L型支架的照片，生成了一個171,504節點的四面體網格，進行了七次分析，涵蓋三個邊界條件假設，並進行了符合代碼的評估，顯示出結構失效並量化了重新設計。所有結果均以第一個自主迭代生成的形式呈現，未經手動修正，強調專業工程師必須審查並簽署任何此類分析。
2604.06788.author: Daniel N. Wilke et.al.
